title: Sir Torrent of Portingale
summary: The repository XML is a local representation of University of Michigan CME item CME00056, manually keyed from Erich Adam's 1887 EETS edition. The XML includes Adam's title page, edited reading text (printed pp. 1-92), and seven early-print fragments (pp. 93-100), but omits his introduction, notes, glossary, and index. Adam retranscribed the unique near-complete manuscript, Chetham's Library Mun.A.6.31, fols. 76r-119v, and corrected it with two independent early-sixteenth-century Bodleian print fragments, Halliwell's 1842 edition, and Kölbing's 1884 collation. Complete printed volumes and later comparison editions are cached with exact internal mappings. Manchester exposes all 768 published manuscript surfaces, including covers and endleaves, but its anti-systematic-extraction terms prevent a compliant bulk cache; the complete viewer, exact work range, and authorized whole-object reproduction route are recorded instead.
review_notes: The intellectual work, physical witnesses, early printed fragments, scholarly editions, digital encodings, scans, and provider image terms are modeled as separate layers.

A complete source means the complete provider-published physical or bibliographic object. Cached edition and journal files retain preliminaries, apparatus, adjacent catalog matter, and non-target pages.
No Chetham images were cached because Manchester's current terms prohibit systematic extraction or reutilization of substantial parts of the site.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00056
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion workflow and supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00056.
The source file entered the CME repository in commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c on 2024-11-24.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00056
entities.description: A manually keyed, proofread, and TEI-like digital representation of selected matter from Adam's 1887 edition.
notes: The XML contains one title-page division, a 2,677-L main poem division, and a 234-L addendum containing seven print-fragment groups.
Its 101 PB elements point to Adam's title page and printed pp. 1-100; Adam's pp. 101-120 and roman-numbered introduction are not encoded.

entities.label: Erich Adam's 1887 Torrent of Portyngale

bibliographic.citation: E. (Erich) Adam, ed., Torrent of Portyngale: Re-edited from the Unique MS. in the Chetham Library, Manchester, EETS Extra Series 51 (London: published for the Early English Text Society by N. Trübner & Co., 1887), xxxiv + 120 pp.
bibliographic.title: Torrent of Portyngale: Re-edited from the Unique MS. in the Chetham Library, Manchester
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Extra Series 51
bibliographic.publisher: N. Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society

notes: This is the immediate printed source represented by CME00056.
The edited poem occupies pp. 1-92; seven early-print fragments occupy pp. 93-100; notes, glossary, and index occupy pp. 101-120.

Adam says he entered most conjectural restorations into the text, retained manuscript orthography as accurately as possible, expanded contractions in italics, and used the print fragments in difficult passages.

Adam states that Eugen Kölbing revised the introduction, notes, and glossary before and after printing and repeatedly proofread the text; Joseph Hall also checked proofs against the Chetham manuscript.

entities.label: Kraus 1973 unaltered reprint of Adam's 1887 edition

date_statements.label: reprint publication
date_statements.notes: The added Kraus title leaf says Millwood, New York, 1973 and the verso calls the volume an unaltered reprint produced with EETS permission.

bibliographic.citation: E. Adam, Torrent of Portyngale, EETS ES 51 (1887; unaltered reprint, Millwood, NY: Kraus Reprint Co., 1973).
bibliographic.title: Torrent of Portyngale
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Extra Series 51

notes: The cached Internet Archive PDF scans this reprint, not a copy lacking the 1973 Kraus leaves; the inherited 1887 text and the added reprint matter are kept distinct for rights purposes.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of the Kraus 1973 Adam reprint
entities.description: A 164-page PDF scan and complete OCR/scan sidecars preserving the Kraus leaves, the entire inherited 1887 edition, blanks, and scan metadata.
entities.reproduction_format: 164-page PDF plus OCR, DjVu XML, scandata, and metadata XML
notes: The medieval poem maps to physical PDF pp. 41-132 and the seven early-print fragments to pp. 133-140; the complete cached files are not cropped to those ranges.

entities.label: James Orchard Halliwell's 1842 Torrent of Portugal

bibliographic.citation: James Orchard Halliwell, ed., Torrent of Portugal: An English Metrical Romance, Now First Published from an Unique Manuscript of the Fifteenth Century, Preserved in the Chetham Library at Manchester (London: John Russell Smith, 1842), xii + 120 pp.
bibliographic.title: Torrent of Portugal: An English Metrical Romance

notes: The first complete modern publication, transcribed from the Chetham manuscript; its appendix prints six early-print fragments from the Bodleian Douce collection.
Halliwell acknowledges C. G. Hulton's assistance during transcription and use of Sir Frederic Madden's independent 1835 transcript.
Its line numbering accidentally jumps from 1720 to 1820; Adam preserves Halliwell-equivalent numbers in brackets after line 1200 to aid comparison.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete bound scan containing Halliwell's 1842 edition
entities.description: A 160-page PDF and complete OCR/scan sidecars preserving Halliwell's title, preliminaries, poem, appendix, blanks, and a later John Grant bookseller catalogue bound after the edition.
entities.reproduction_format: 160-page PDF plus OCR, DjVu XML, scandata, and metadata XML
notes: Halliwell's poem occupies physical PDF pp. 17-128 and his six-fragment appendix pp. 129-136; the appended John Grant catalogue begins at physical p. 137 and remains in the cache.

entities.label: Eugen Kölbing's 1884 Torrent of Portugal collation

bibliographic.citation: Eugen Kölbing, 'Collationen. V. Torrent of Portugal,' Englische Studien 7 (1884): 344-348.
bibliographic.title: Collationen. V. Torrent of Portugal

notes: Kölbing collates Halliwell against the Chetham manuscript, corrects readings and numbering, and prints the seventh early-print fragment omitted by Halliwell; Adam cites it as the source for his fragment VI.

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of Englische Studien volume 7
entities.description: A 514-page complete journal-volume PDF; Kölbing's Torrent collation occupies printed pp. 344-348, physical PDF pp. 356-360.
notes: The exact same cached PDF is relevant to CME00004 and is reused here by true hard link rather than duplicated bytes.

entities.label: Chetham's Library MS Mun.A.6.31
entities.description: The unique near-complete manuscript witness of Sir Torrent, copied on paper in a late-fifteenth-century miscellany. Torrent occupies fols. 76r-119v; the final surface is shared with the opening of the Lamentation of the Virgin.

holding.notes: The LUNA records give current repository Chetham's Library, Manchester and previous accession number 8009.

physical_description.format: Late-fifteenth-century household miscellany; Torrent is written by two hands and has rubricated initials but no separately spaced stanzas
notes: The LUNA parent sequence comprises 768 digital surfaces from upper cover through lower cover, including inner covers, endpapers, flyleaves, blanks, and adjacent works.
Modern analysis assigns Torrent lines 1-1049 to Hand 7 and the remainder to Hand 5.
The final Torrent lines and the following Lamentation of the Virgin share fol. 119v.

entities.label: Manchester LUNA published sequence for Chetham MS Mun.A.6.31
entities.description: A publicly viewable 768-image color sequence covering the repository's published inventory from upper cover through lower cover. It includes inner covers, endpapers, flyleaves, blanks, all manuscript leaves, and adjacent works; no separate spine or edge photographs were found.
entities.reproduction_format: 768 color LUNA image records, each with a single-image IIIF manifest
notes: Sir Torrent maps to LUNA image numbers 163-250, fols. 76r-119v. Image 250 is titled Lamentation of the Virgin because the next work begins on the shared folio after Torrent's explicit.

No local copy was made: current terms allow limited personal/non-commercial extracts but prohibit systematic extraction or reutilization of substantial parts and publication of a database containing substantial parts.
Coverage is complete only relative to the 768-surface published LUNA inventory; spine and edge views are not exposed.

entities.label: Richard Pynson Sir Torrent fragment, c. 1505
entities.description: A two-leaf early printed proof fragment preserving passages of Sir Torrent, now associated in current scholarship with Bodleian Douce Fragment e.20(1), STC 24133. It belongs to a transmission branch independent of the Chetham manuscript.

holding.notes: The current physical SOLO record and Wade's bibliography do not align cleanly on printer and sub-number; see the open question.

physical_description.format: Early printed proof fragment on the reverse of a broadside associated with John Skelton's elegy on Henry VII

entities.label: Wynkyn de Worde Sir Torrent fragment, c. 1510
entities.description: A two-leaf early printed proof fragment preserving passages of Sir Torrent, now associated in current scholarship with Bodleian Douce Fragment e.20(2), STC 24133.5. It belongs to a transmission branch independent of the Chetham manuscript.

holding.notes: The current physical SOLO record and Wade's bibliography do not align cleanly on printer and sub-number; see the open question.

physical_description.format: Early printed proof fragment on the reverse of a broadside associated with John Skelton's elegy on Henry VII

entities.label: James Wade's 2017 Sir Torrent of Portingale

bibliographic.citation: James Wade, ed., Sir Torrent of Portingale (Kalamazoo, MI: Medieval Institute Publications, 2017), Middle English Texts Series.
bibliographic.title: Sir Torrent of Portingale

notes: A later comparison edition, not an ancestor of CME00056.
Wade follows the Chetham manuscript as copy-text, adopts print-fragment readings when strongly supported by rhyme, meter, or line order, and documents emendations in textual notes.
The cached 127-page PDF preserves the complete edition: introduction, 2,671-line text, explanatory and textual notes, bibliography, and glossary.

entities.label: Project Gutenberg ebook 35190, Torrent of Portyngale
entities.description: A complete public HTML/EPUB/plain-text re-encoding of Adam's 1887 edition produced from Google Print scans by Distributed Proofreaders.
notes: The cached HTML package preserves Adam's introduction, complete edited poem, seven fragments, notes, glossary, index, and small image assets; the work text begins at the #torrent anchor and the parallel fragments at #fragments.

subject.description: Adam's newly retranscribed and collated edition

scope.notes: Adam calls his work a re-edition, compares Halliwell throughout, and preserves a numbering crosswalk, but independently retranscribes the manuscript rather than merely resetting Halliwell.
Adam uses the two early-print objects jointly; the exact allocation of his seven numbered fragment groups between them is unresolved.
Adam uses the two early-print objects jointly; the exact allocation of his seven numbered fragment groups between them is unresolved.
Adam explicitly cites Kölbing's article for the seventh fragment and acknowledges Kölbing's extensive revision and proofreading.

access.access_method: Use Manchester Imaging Services to request an authorized complete-object surrogate; specify every manuscript leaf, covers/boards, inner covers and pastedowns, endpapers, flyleaves, blanks, spine, and edges where offered, and identify fols. 76r-119v separately as the work portion
access.contact: University of Manchester Library Imaging Services; quote Chetham's Library MS Mun.A.6.31 (formerly MS 8009)
access.format: Repository-supplied digital images; requested delivery and publication permissions depend on quotation
notes: The existing viewer already publishes 768 surfaces, but an authorized retained whole-object delivery is needed before a local cache can be recorded.

Ask separately about private research storage, repository publication, commercial print-on-demand use, attribution, and whether the requested delivery may include or redistribute images not covered by the collection-level CC statement.

rights.component: Local XML representation of the U-M/CME keyed text
rights.basis: The source collection applies No Copyright-United States to the CME materials represented by the local XML; no broader conclusion is drawn from the local copy alone.
notes: No Copyright-United States is jurisdiction-scoped and does not itself constitute a worldwide license.

rights.component: University of Michigan CME digital text and collection materials
rights.basis: The collection says its texts were manually keyed from public-domain editions and labels the materials No Copyright-United States.
notes: This is a jurisdiction-qualified provider assertion, not a worldwide dedication.

rights.basis: The edition was published in 1887; Internet Archive metadata reports NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT in the United States.

rights.component: 1973 Kraus title leaves, reprint presentation, and any edition-specific matter
rights.basis: The scan contains added 1973 Kraus matter even though Internet Archive describes the item as 1887 and reports NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT; no item-specific permission for the added matter was found.
notes: The public-domain 1887 content and the later reprint wrapper are separate rights layers.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan files and derivatives
rights.jurisdiction: United States; provider delivery terms separate
rights.basis: Internet Archive metadata asserts possible-copyright-status NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT for the item, but identifies it as the 1887 work and does not address the visible 1973 Kraus leaves.
notes: Local caching does not establish permission to redistribute the provider-generated PDF or later reprint matter.

rights.basis: The edition was published in 1842; Internet Archive metadata reports NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT in the United States.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan files and derivatives, including the later bookseller catalogue

notes: Local caching does not itself establish redistribution permission for the scan files.

rights.jurisdiction: United States; provider delivery terms separate
rights.basis: The complete 1884 volume is publicly delivered by Internet Archive; no item-specific open license for the derivative was verified.
notes: Local caching and hard-link reuse do not establish public redistribution permission for the PDF.

rights.basis: The anonymous work and fifteenth-century witness predate modern copyright; image and access conditions are analyzed separately.

rights.component: Manchester LUNA image records for Chetham MS Mun.A.6.31
rights.jurisdiction: Worldwide license statement; item-specific rights-holder qualification
rights.basis: Manchester's support page says University of Manchester images are released under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0, but warns that some collections have different restrictions and directs users to item Rights Holder fields; these records name Chetham's Library as rights holder for image and work.
notes: The collection-level license and item-level Chetham attribution are preserved together rather than treating either as silently controlling every use.

rights.component: Contractual use of Manchester Digital Collections content and database
rights.basis: Site terms allow personal, non-commercial extracts but prohibit systematic extraction or reutilization of substantial parts, commercial use without license, and publication of a database containing substantial parts.
notes: These terms are why no whole-manuscript image bundle was cached despite technical image availability.

rights.basis: The fragment was printed around 1505; Bodleian reproduction terms and EEBO access remain separate.
The fragment was printed around 1510; Bodleian reproduction terms and EEBO access remain separate.

Bodleian says supplied images may be used for non-commercial and academic purposes under its terms; non-academic electronic or print publication requires a separate permission application, even when underlying material is public domain.
notes: A private research delivery does not automatically authorize Git publication or commercial print-on-demand use.

rights.basis: Bodleian says supplied images may be used for non-commercial and academic purposes under its terms; non-academic electronic or print publication requires a separate permission application, even when underlying material is public domain.
notes: A private research delivery does not automatically authorize Git publication or commercial print-on-demand use.

rights.component: 2017 editorial text, notes, PDF, and METS digital presentation
rights.basis: METS states that digital editions are for individual use only and asks users to contact the Executive Director to reprint digital text; it separately licenses XML CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.
notes: The cached PDF is a private research source and must not be treated as a redistributable corpus asset. The XML license does not automatically govern the PDF.

rights.component: Project Gutenberg ebook 35190 and its package
rights.basis: The ebook record explicitly says Public domain in the USA and supplies the Project Gutenberg terms route.
notes: Users outside the United States must assess local law; Project Gutenberg trademark/license conditions remain separate from the public-domain text.

editorial_practices.description: Adam states that manuscript contractions are expanded and that the expansions are printed in italics.
notes: Italic type therefore carries editorial expansion data and should not be interpreted as manuscript emphasis or as a facsimile of the original abbreviation mark.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Corrupt or metrically defective Chetham readings and independent early-print variants
editorial_practices.representation: Conjectures and print-fragment readings entered into the main text, with alternatives and discussion in notes
editorial_practices.description: Adam says many conjectures were necessary and most were entered directly into the text; he consulted the print fragments in difficult passages and quantified where their readings were preferable, equal, uncertain, or inferior.

editorial_practices.representation: Capitals introduced at new periods and for proper names
editorial_practices.description: Adam explicitly states this normalization while otherwise aiming to reproduce manuscript orthography.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Complete Adam volume: title, introduction, poem, seven fragments, notes, glossary, and index
editorial_practices.representation: Title page, poem, and seven fragments encoded; introduction, notes after p. 100, glossary, and index omitted
editorial_practices.description: The XML's page-break sequence and divisions stop at printed p. 100 although the complete edition continues through p. 120 and includes xxxiv introductory pages.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Adam's italic typographic distinction for expanded contractions
editorial_practices.representation: 1,394 HI elements and 139 ABBR elements in the repository XML
editorial_practices.description: The encoding preserves extensive italic spans from the printed edition. Those spans represent Adam's editorial typography, not the manuscript's original abbreviation shapes.
notes: The counts are encoding metrics; a reading-level audit would be required before claiming every HI is an expanded abbreviation.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Chetham copy-text, two early-print fragments, and earlier editorial readings
editorial_practices.representation: Manuscript-based text with selected fragment readings and explicit textual notes
editorial_practices.description: Wade adopts print-fragment readings when overwhelmingly persuasive for rhyme, meter, or line order and documents emendations in textual notes.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Chetham manuscript spelling, word division, capitalization, punctuation, and i/j and u/v graphs
editorial_practices.representation: Expanded contractions, regularized word division, modern capitalization and punctuation, and normalized i/j and u/v
editorial_practices.description: Wade's editorial statement distinguishes these reader-facing normalizations from substantive emendation and lacuna markers.

evidence.citation: University of Manchester Library, Imaging Services, Order an image; and Public Sector Information reuse service.
evidence.summary: Provides the authorized reproduction-order route and distinguishes imaging charges and publication/reuse questions.

evidence.citation: James Wade, ed., Sir Torrent of Portingale, Middle English Texts Series (2017).
evidence.locator: introduction, editorial statement, text, textual notes, bibliography
evidence.summary: Current scholarly evidence for anonymous authorship, composition toward the end of the fourteenth century, 2,671-line twelve-line tail-rhyme form, Chetham copy-text and hands, fragment use, genre, themes, likely partial relationship to Sir Eglamour, and editorial normalization.

evidence.citation: James Wade, Sir Torrent of Portingale, Bibliography: Manuscript and Early Printed Editions.
evidence.summary: Identifies Chetham MS 8009 [Mun.A.6.31], Richard Pynson c. 1505? as Bodleian Douce Fragment e.20(1), STC 24133, and Wynkyn de Worde c. 1510? as Douce Fragment e.20(2), STC 24133.5.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series, Copyright & Citation.
evidence.summary: States that digital editions are for individual use only and reprinting requires contact with the Executive Director; separately licenses XML under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0.

evidence.citation: Database of Middle English Romance, Sir Torrent of Portyngale.
evidence.summary: Catalogs composition c. 1400 in the North Midlands, twelve-line tail-rhyme, manuscript MS 8009 fols. 76r-119v dated 1470-1480, two two-leaf early prints, modern editions, keywords, and a detailed plot summary based on Adam.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries SOLO records associated with Douce Fragment e.20 and the Sir Torrent proofs.
evidence.summary: The physical record describes a cropped proof of four Torrent pages on the reverse of the Skelton broadside and gives a Weston Library request route, but its current de Worde attribution and e.20(1) citation conflict with Wade's separate Pynson e.20(1)/de Worde e.20(2) bibliography. The two additional records are vendor/bibliographic descriptions and do not fully resolve the physical crosswalk.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries Imaging Services, Order images; Special Collections copying; Reproducing images.
evidence.summary: Provides high-resolution image and mediated scan request routes and states that non-commercial and academic use is covered by image terms, while non-academic electronic or print publication requires separate permission; underlying public-domain status does not cancel provider terms.

evidence.citation: Folger catalog bridges to ProQuest EEBO records for the Pynson and de Worde Sir Torrent fragments.
evidence.summary: Identifies subscription-mediated EEBO reproductions associated with Early English Books 1475-1640 reel 1737:12; anonymous full-image access and complete physical-object coverage could not be audited.

evidence.citation: Project Gutenberg ebook 35190, Torrent of Portyngale by E. Adam.
evidence.summary: Identifies Adam as editor, credits Distributed Proofreaders and Google Print scans, gives release/update dates and exact formats, and asserts public-domain status in the United States. The cached ZIP contains the complete HTML edition and four image assets.

open_questions.question: Will Chetham's Library or Manchester Imaging Services supply an authorized retained complete-object surrogate of Mun.A.6.31, including every leaf and all covers, boards, spine, pastedowns, endpapers, flyleaves, blanks, and edges where available, with explicit research and publication terms?
next_steps: Request the complete physical manuscript rather than fols. 76r-119v alone and identify that folio range separately as the work portion.
Ask whether the delivery may be stored in the private Git-ignored cache and whether any images may later be published in the repository or used in commercial print-on-demand research outputs.
If authorized files arrive, preserve every delivered surface, record exact source URLs or order identifiers, hash the files, and add a complete or explicitly qualified local_copies record.
notes: The public viewer is complete relative to its 768-surface inventory, but current anti-extraction terms prevent bulk caching; no spine or edge photographs are exposed.

open_questions.question: How do the current physical SOLO record, Wade's e.20(1)/e.20(2) bibliography, STC 24133/24133.5, and Adam's seven numbered fragment groups map object by object?
next_steps: Ask Bodleian Special Collections to confirm the current shelfmark, printer attribution, and complete recto/verso inventory for each two-leaf object.
Compare authorized whole-object images against Adam pp. 93-100 and Wade's F.I-F.VII textual notes.
Update the two fragment relations with exact object-specific passage ranges once the crosswalk is verified.
notes: The current manifest deliberately does not invent which of Adam's seven fragments belongs to which printed object.

open_questions.question: Does the collection-level CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 statement govern the Chetham images despite item fields naming Chetham's Library as rights holder and site terms restricting systematic extraction and transmission?
next_steps: Request written clarification from Manchester Imaging Services and Chetham's Library for this exact shelfmark.
Ask separately about private research storage, non-commercial Git publication, and commercial or print-on-demand use.
notes: Until clarified, no whole sequence should be downloaded or redistributed from the public viewer.
